# xcli v0.1.0

The first public release of xcli provides one entry point for installing, authenticating with, running, switching between, and serially orchestrating mainstream AI coding agent CLIs.

## Highlights

- Built-in adapters for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode.
- Declarative aliases and custom argv-based agents without shell evaluation.
- Interactive passthrough and one-shot runs with native argument forwarding.
- Strict user-level YAML configuration with deterministic environment and proxy profiles.
- Serial YAML workflows with explicit output references, retries, timeouts, and cancellation.
- Private run metadata with optional output recording.
- Safe npm and Homebrew installer selection without `sudo` or `curl | sh` pipelines.

## Install

Download the archive for your platform, verify it against `checksums.txt`, extract the `xcli` binary, and place it on your `PATH`.

This release contains archives for macOS and Linux on amd64 and arm64. The macOS binaries are not code-signed or notarized.

## Boundaries

xcli manages real agent CLI processes. Authentication, credentials, permissions, plugins, MCP settings, and vendor-specific advanced arguments remain owned by each native agent.

Parallel orchestration, automatic routing, cost aggregation, process control, session resume, Windows support, and a web UI are intentionally deferred beyond v0.1.
